---
title: "Mapping Workflow"
author: "AY"
---

This page documents the current mapping workflow in this project.

## Overview

- Genome download and indexing are handled by `./scripts/bcfngs genomes ...`. See [updateing_genomes](updating_genomes.qmd) for more.
- Mapping is implemented in [`workflow/mapping.smk`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/workflow/mapping.smk).
- The [`./scripts/bcfngs`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/scripts/bcfngs) is used with the subcommand `mapping plan` for validation and dry-run and `mapping run` for execution.
- The workflow supports `star`, `bwa`, and `bowtie2`. Further mapping tools are coming ...
- For every selected mapper, the workflow creates BAM, BAM index, and bigWig output files.

## Required CLI settings

All available parameters can be listed using the `--help` command.

```bash
$ ./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan|run --help
usage: bcfngs mapping plan [-h] [--mapper MAPPER] [--paired-end | --single-end] [--project PROJECT] [--input FASTQ_DIR] [--output OUTDIR]
                           [--organism ORGANISM] [--threads THREADS] [--ram-bytes RAM_BYTES] [--star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION]
                           [--bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION] [--bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION] [--keep-intermediary-files]
                           [--bw-bin-size BW_BIN_SIZE] [--bw-normalization BW_NORMALIZATION] [--executor {local,slurm}] [--jobs JOBS]
                           [--latency-wait LATENCY_WAIT] [--slurm-partition SLURM_PARTITION] [--slurm-time SLURM_TIME]
                           [--slurm-mail-user SLURM_MAIL_USER] [--slurm-mail-type SLURM_MAIL_TYPE] [--slurm-account SLURM_ACCOUNT]
                           [--slurm-cpus-per-task SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK] [--slurm-star-mem-mb SLURM_STAR_MEM_MB]
                           [--slurm-aligner-mem-mb SLURM_ALIGNER_MEM_MB] [--slurm-index-mem-mb SLURM_INDEX_MEM_MB]
                           [--slurm-bigwig-cpus SLURM_BIGWIG_CPUS] [--slurm-bigwig-mem-mb SLURM_BIGWIG_MEM_MB]

```

Per-run settings are passed on the command line:

- `--project`: project identifier used in the output layout. Default: `P000`
- `--input`: FASTQ input directory. Default: current working directory
- `--output`: output root directory. Default: current working directory
- `--organism`: one organism key from [`configs/genomes.yaml`](/Users/yeroslaviz/Documents/Github/bcfngs_Mapping_automation/configs/genomes.yaml). **This is required.**
- `--threads`: mapping threads per job. Default: `32`
- `--ram-bytes`: STAR BAM sort memory limit. Default: `168632718037`

If `--organism` is omitted, the wrapper prints the configured organism list (same as `--list-org`) and exits.

Common optional flags include:

- `--mapper`
- `--star-index-version`
- `--bwa-index-version`
- `--bowtie2-index-version`
- `--paired-end` or `--single-end`

## Required genome resources

Before mapping starts, the workflow checks that the needed indices and chromosome size file already exist under the configured genomes root.

Depending on the mapper selection, it expects:

- default `star`: `<genomes_root>/<organism>/starIndex/SA`
- default `bwa`: `<genomes_root>/<organism>/bwaIndex/<organism>.bwt`
- default `bowtie2`: `<genomes_root>/<organism>/bowtie2Index/<organism>.1.bt2`
- explicit versions: `<genomes_root>/<organism>/<assembly>-starIndex-<version>/...`, `<assembly>-bwaIndex-<version>/...`, `<assembly>-bowtie2Index-<version>/...`
- all runs: `<genomes_root>/<organism>.chromSize`

If one of these files is missing, the run stops before any mapping jobs are submitted.

If the resolved mapper binary version does not match the stable symlink target, the wrapper now fails before submission and asks you to pass the matching explicit version flag.
If `configs/genomes.yaml` was updated to a different assembly than the last built indices, the wrapper also stops and asks you to rebuild the genome resources first.

## FASTQ pattern auto-detection

The workflow detects the input pattern directly from the FASTQ directory. It can work with both compressed `.fastq.gz` and uncompressed `.fastq` inputs are supported. The supported R1 patterns are:

1. `*_R1.fastq` or `*_R1.fastq.gz`
2. `*_R1_001.fastq` or `*_R1_001.fastq.gz`
3. `*_L1_R1.fastq` or `*_L1_R1.fastq.gz`
4. `*.conc.R1.fastq` or `*.conc.R1.fastq.gz`

For paired-end mode, the matching R2 files must also exist.

## Output layout

Outputs are written below:

```text
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/
```

For each selected mapper, the workflow creates:

```text
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/bigwig/<sample>.bw
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.counts.tab
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.SJ.out.tab

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/bigwig/<sample>.bw

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/bigwig/<sample>.bw

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/logs/
```

## Visualization tracks with `BigWig`

BigWig files are generated with the `BamCoverage` subcommand from the [deeptools](https://deeptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) suite.

## Basic usage

### Dry-run and validation

Use `plan` first. This validates config and inputs, runs Snakemake lint, and performs a dry-run.

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --mapper star \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --input /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --output /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
```

Example with an explicitly selected older STAR index:

We first must make sure that the older version of the tool as a module is preloaded.

```bash
module load star/2.7.10b
```

Then, we can execute this command:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules mapping plan \
  --mapper star \
  --star-index-version 2.7.10b \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --input /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --output /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
```

First the tool check for the loaded versions of the needed tools:

```
Resolved tool versions:
  - python: micromamba 3.12.13 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/python)
  - snakemake: micromamba 8.30.0 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/snakemake)
  - samtools: micromamba 1.23.1 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/samtools)
  - bamCoverage: micromamba 3.5.6 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/bamCoverage)
  - STAR: star/2.7.10b 2.7.10b (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/STAR/2.7.10b/STAR)
```

We can see that here, the STAR version is taken from the module, 2.7.10b.

### Local run

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --mapper star \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --input /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --output /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
  --executor local \
  --jobs 20
```

### SLURM run

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --mapper star,bwa \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --input /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --output /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --slurm-partition p.hpcl93 \
  --slurm-time 04:00:00 \
  --slurm-cpus-per-task 20
```

## Direct Snakemake usage

The workflow can also be called directly:

```bash
snakemake \
  --printshellcmds \
  --dry-run \
  -s workflow/mapping.smk \
  --config \
    project=P000 \
    organism=Caenorhabditis_elegans \
    fastq_dir=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
    outdir=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
    mappers=star,bwa \
    read_mode=paired \
    threads=16 \
    ram_bytes=168632718037 \
    bw_bin_size=10 \
    bw_normalization=CPM
```

## Notes

- The workflow supports exactly one organism per run.
- Unknown mapper names are rejected.
- Duplicate mapper names are removed automatically.
- The default read mode is paired-end. Use `--single-end` explicitly for single-end data.
- STAR keeps the full STAR output bundle, including the STAR-generated `.out` files and STAR log files.
- bigWig files are produced directly with `bamCoverage`. The old wig conversion step is no longer part of this workflow.
- Run `./scripts/bcfngs env check` first if you want to verify that `snakemake`, `STAR`, `bwa`, `bowtie2-build`, `samtools`, and `bamCoverage` are available in the configured environment. With `--profile modules`, the wrapper also prints the resolved tool source/version report to `stderr`.

## Useful flags

- `--mapper`: comma-separated mapper list such as `star,bwa,bowtie2`
- `--star-index-version`, `--bwa-index-version`, `--bowtie2-index-version`: require matching versioned indices instead of the stable symlinks
- `--input`, `--output`: FASTQ input directory and output root directory
- `--threads`: mapping threads per job, default `32`
- `--ram-bytes`: STAR `--limitBAMsortRAM`, default `168632718037`
- `--bw-bin-size`: set `bamCoverage --binSize`
- `--bw-normalization`: set `bamCoverage --normalizeUsing`
- `--jobs`: maximum concurrent jobs
- `--executor`: choose `local` or `slurm`
